title: Hemifacial Microsomia / Goldenhar Spectrum

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Second most common craniofacial anomaly**

• 1/3,500-5,000 births
• Spectrum of severity
• Usually sporadic
• First and second branchial arch derivatives affected

**Terminology:**
• Hemifacial microsomia (HFM)
• Craniofacial microsomia
• Oculoauriculovertebral spectrum (OAVS)
• Goldenhar syndrome (with epibulbar dermoid + vertebral)

sections.title: Clinical Features
sections.content:
**Mandible:**
• Hypoplasia (variable severity)
• Absent condyle/ramus in severe cases
• Facial asymmetry

**Ear:**
• Microtia spectrum
• Preauricular tags
• Aural atresia
• Conductive hearing loss

**Soft Tissue:**
• Facial nerve weakness
• Macrostomia (lateral oral cleft)
• Parotid hypoplasia
• Masticatory muscle hypoplasia

**Eye:**
• Epibulbar dermoid (Goldenhar)
• Upper lid coloboma

**Spine (Goldenhar):**
• Hemivertebrae
• Cervical fusion

sections.title: Classification
sections.content:
**OMENS Classification:**
• **O**rbit
• **M**andible (I-III)
• **E**ar
• **N**erve (facial)
• **S**oft tissue

**Pruzansky/Kaban Mandible:**

**Type I:**
• Small but normal shape
• All components present

**Type IIA:**
• Abnormal shape
• Functional TMJ

**Type IIB:**
• Abnormal shape
• Non-functional TMJ

**Type III:**
• Absent ramus/condyle/TMJ

sections.title: Management
sections.content:
**Timing Principles:**
• Address functional issues first (airway, hearing)
• Bony surgery after growth assessment
• Soft tissue follows bone

**Hearing:**
• Early amplification
• BAHA if canal atresia

**Mandible:**
• Type I/IIA: May observe
• Type IIB/III: Distraction osteogenesis or costochondral graft
• Orthognathic surgery at skeletal maturity

**Ear:**
• Microtia reconstruction ~age 6-10
• Rib cartilage framework
• Or prosthetic ear

**Soft Tissue:**
• Fat grafting
• Free tissue transfer for severe cases

keyTakeaways: Second most common craniofacial anomaly after cleft
Spectrum of severity - use OMENS classification
Screen for cervical spine anomalies (intubation risk)
Mandible treatment depends on severity type

reviewQuestions.question: A child with hemifacial microsomia is scheduled for surgery. What screening is important before intubation?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemifacial microsomia (especially Goldenhar variant) is associated with cervical spine anomalies including hemivertebrae and fusions. Cervical spine imaging is important before intubation to assess for instability and plan airway management.
